response: Implemented in May 2018, the EU’s General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) mandates strict data handling rules for any organization processing EU residents' information, including rights to access, erasure, and explicit consent, alongside a mandatory 72-hour breach reporting window. Noncompliance carries severe financial penalties of up to 4% of global annual revenue or €20 million, with major fines already issued to Meta (€1.2 billion), Amazon (€746 million), and Google (over €200 million). Despite criticisms regarding heavy compliance burdens and inconsistent enforcement, the GDPR has successfully established data privacy as a fundamental right and set a global legislative standard that has inspired similar data protection laws worldwide.
